title: MS
biography: Wilson Greatbatch was an American engineer who invented the implantable cardiac pacemaker and the lithium-iodide battery cell to power it. His pacemaker has saved millions of lives.
aiSummary: Wilson Greatbatch is a biomedical engineering specialist at University at Buffalo. Based in Buffalo.
